name: verblaze
documentation: Verblaze is an automatic localization generation tool that helps you manage and generate translations for your projects. 📚 **[Visit verblaze.com](https://verblaze.com)** for detailed guides and examples. ## Features - 🚀 Quick and easy setup - 🌍 Support for multiple frameworks and technologies - 📦 Automatic string extraction - 🔄 Seamless integration with Verblaze Dashboard - 💻 Simple command-line interface ## Installation ```bash pip install verblaze ``` ## Quick Start ### 1. Configure Verblaze First, set up your Verblaze configuration using your secret key from the dashboard: ```bash verblaze config --secret-key YOUR_SECRET_KEY ``` ### 2. Generate Translations Extract strings from your project using the generate command: ```bash verblaze generate -t TEMPLATE -d PROJECT_DIRECTORY -f FOLDERS ``` #### Parameters: - `-t`: Template/framework (Required) - Supported templates: flutter, react, react-native, angular, plain-html, vue, svelte, ember, backbone, swift, kotlin, javafx, wpf, qt, blazor, nextjs - `-d`: Project directory path (Required) - `-f`: Folders containing UI code, comma-separated (Required) #### Example: ```bash verblaze generate -t react -d /path/to/project -f "src,components,pages" ``` ## Supported Frameworks - React - React Native - Angular - Vue - Svelte - Flutter - Plain HTML - Ember - Backbone - Swift - Kotlin - JavaFX - WPF - Qt - Blazor - Next.js ## License This project is licensed under the MIT License. See the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details. ## Contact - Website: [verblaze.com](https://verblaze.com) - Email: support@verblaze.com - [Github](https://github.com/Verblaze) ## FAQ **Q: How do I get my secret key?** A: You can obtain your secret key from the Verblaze Dashboard after creating an account. **Q: Which Python versions are supported?** A: Python 3.6 and above are supported. **Q: Can I use Verblaze with my existing project?** A: Yes, Verblaze supports multiple frameworks and can be integrated with existing projects. ## Changelog ### [0.0.4] - 2024-11-27 - Added loading animation during string synchronization - Fixed regex pattern for Flutter string extraction - Improved user feedback during synchronization process - Updated documentation and README ### [0.0.1] - 2024-11-20 - Initial release - Added support for multiple frameworks - Implemented string extraction functionality - Created CLI interface with config and generate commands

modules.name: verblaze.file_utils
documentation: file_utils.py

functions.name: verblaze.file_utils.get_actual_path
documentation:
Returns the actual path within the project where the code resides,
based on the technology/template used.

functions.name: verblaze.file_utils.get_file_extension_from_template
documentation: Returns the file extensions associated with the given template.

functions.name: verblaze.file_utils.list_all_files_in_directory
documentation:
Lists all files in the given directory and its subdirectories that match the specified
template and are located within the specified folders.

documentation: Verblaze: Auto-Localization Generation Tool

documentation: Generate localization files for the given project.
generate komutu

documentation: Set configuration values for Verblaze.
config komutu (tek seferde tanımlandı)

modules.name: verblaze.string_extractors
documentation: string_extractors.py

functions.name: verblaze.string_extractors.register_extractor
documentation: Decorator to register extractor classes in the EXTRACTOR_REGISTRY.

functions.name: verblaze.string_extractors.get_string_extractor
documentation: Retrieves the appropriate string extractor class for the given template.
Additional extractor classes can be added here following the same pattern.

classes.name: verblaze.string_extractors.BaseStringExtractor
documentation:
Base class for string extractors. All extractor classes should inherit from this class
and implement the extract_strings method.

classes.name: verblaze.string_extractors.DartStringExtractor
documentation: Extracts strings from Dart (.dart) files in Flutter projects.

classes.name: verblaze.string_extractors.ReactStringExtractor
documentation: Extracts strings from JSX and TSX files in React and React Native projects.

classes.name: verblaze.string_extractors.NextJsStringExtractor
documentation: Extracts strings from Next.js (.js, .jsx, .ts, .tsx) files.

classes.name: verblaze.string_extractors.AngularStringExtractor
documentation: Extracts strings from TypeScript (.ts) and HTML files in Angular projects.

classes.name: verblaze.string_extractors.PlainHtmlStringExtractor
documentation: Extracts text content from HTML files.

classes.name: verblaze.string_extractors.VueStringExtractor
